questions.text: Which of the following elements is an alkali metal?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is in Group 1, the alkali metals. Mg, Ca are alkaline earth metals, Al is a p-block element.

questions.text: Alkali metals are characterized by their tendency to:
questions.explanation: They have one valence electron which they readily lose to form +1 ions.

questions.text: Which of the following alkali metals has the lowest ionization enthalpy?
questions.explanation: Ionization enthalpy decreases down the group due to increasing atomic size and shielding effect.

questions.text: Alkaline earth metals are found in which group of the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Group 2 elements are called alkaline earth metals.

questions.text: Which of the following alkaline earth metals has the highest hydration enthalpy?
questions.explanation: Hydration enthalpy decreases down the group due to increasing ionic size.

questions.text: The general electronic configuration of s-block elements is:
questions.explanation: Group 1 elements have ns¹ and Group 2 elements have ns².

questions.text: Which alkali metal is stored under kerosene oil to prevent reaction with air and moisture?
questions.explanation: Sodium is highly reactive and reacts vigorously with oxygen and water.

questions.text: The diagonal relationship exists between Lithium and:
questions.explanation: Li and Mg show similar properties due to similar charge/radius ratio.

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is used as an antacid?
questions.explanation: Magnesium hydroxide (Milk of Magnesia) is a common antacid.

questions.text: Plaster of Paris is chemically:
questions.explanation: CaSO₄·½H₂O. It is used in casts and sculptures.

questions.text: The flame color produced by Sodium is:
questions.explanation: Characteristic flame test color for Na.

questions.text: Which of the following is an anhydrous salt?
questions.explanation: Dead burnt plaster is anhydrous CaSO₄, formed by strong heating of gypsum.

questions.text: The solubility of alkaline earth metal hydroxides _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Lattice energy decreases more slowly than hydration energy.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the Solvay process for the manufacture of sodium carbonate?
questions.explanation: Brine (NaCl solution) is a key raw material.

questions.text: Beryllium shows anomalous behavior compared to other alkaline earth metals due to:
questions.explanation: These factors contribute to its covalent character and unique properties.

questions.text: Which of the following is a component of chlorophyll?
questions.explanation: Magnesium is the central metal ion in the chlorophyll molecule.

questions.text: Lithium reacts with nitrogen to form:
questions.explanation: Lithium is the only alkali metal that directly reacts with nitrogen to form a nitride.

questions.text: The reducing power of alkali metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Ease of losing electrons increases, making them stronger reducing agents.

questions.text: Which of the following is known as 'milk of magnesia'?
questions.explanation: A suspension of magnesium hydroxide in water.

questions.text: The stability of peroxides and superoxides of alkali metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Larger cations stabilize larger anions (peroxide O₂²⁻, superoxide O₂⁻).

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the manufacture of cement?
questions.explanation: Limestone (CaCO₃) is a primary raw material for cement production.

questions.text: The biological importance of sodium ions includes:
questions.explanation: Na⁺ and K⁺ ions play crucial roles in biological systems.

questions.text: Which of the following is a strong base?
questions.explanation: NaOH is a strong alkali. Mg(OH)₂ and Be(OH)₂ are weaker bases.

questions.text: The flame color produced by Calcium is:
questions.explanation: Characteristic flame test color for Ca.

questions.text: The density of alkali metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Atomic mass increases more rapidly than atomic volume.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the treatment of bipolar disorders?
questions.explanation: Lithium carbonate is a common medication for bipolar disorder.

questions.text: The melting points of alkali metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Due to decreasing metallic bonding strength.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a super-oxide?
questions.explanation: Superoxides contain the O₂⁻ ion.

questions.text: The reducing power of alkaline earth metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Ease of losing electrons increases.

questions.text: Which of the following is used as a drying agent?
questions.explanation: Both calcium chloride and calcium oxide are effective drying agents.

questions.text: The basic character of hydroxides of alkaline earth metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Due to increasing ionic character and decreasing electronegativity.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in photography?
questions.explanation: Silver bromide is light-sensitive and used in photographic films.

questions.text: The hydration enthalpy of alkali metal ions _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Due to increasing ionic size.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in street lights?
questions.explanation: Sodium vapor lamps produce a characteristic golden-yellow light.

questions.text: The thermal stability of carbonates of alkaline earth metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Larger cations stabilize larger anions (CO₃²⁻).

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the preparation of hydrogen?
questions.explanation: Calcium hydride (hydrolith) reacts with water to produce hydrogen.

questions.text: The flame color produced by Barium is:
questions.explanation: Characteristic flame test color for Ba.

questions.text: Which of the following is used as a flux in metallurgy?
questions.explanation: Limestone and quicklime are used to remove impurities in metal extraction.

questions.text: The density of alkaline earth metals _____ down the group.
questions.explanation: Atomic mass increases more rapidly than atomic volume.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the treatment of cancer?
questions.explanation: Radium (a radioactive alkaline earth metal) was historically used in radiotherapy.
